covered_sentences: Hush, said Hareton, afraid of making Heathcliff angry.
Get out of my sight, you little witch! cried Heathcliff.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Two-shot composition: teenage boy Hareton stands center-left facing right, one hand half-raised in a hushing gesture, body angled defensively toward the looming figure of Heathcliff at center-right; low camera angle looking slightly up at Heathcliff to exaggerate his imposing height; tense diagonal axis between the two figures; negative space behind Hareton opens toward the door.

Hareton (C5) raises one rough hand palm-outward in a cautionary "hush" gesture, his broad shoulders slightly hunched inward, weight shifted back as if bracing — conveying nervous appeasement rather than defiance. Heathcliff (C1) stands fully upright, chin lifted, one arm extended with index finger pointing sharply toward the side door — his posture radiates cold command and barely contained fury; no physical contact between figures.

Interior of the Wuthering Heights parlor (S1): stone walls frame the background, the large stone fireplace visible at right emitting dim orange coals, dark wooden beams overhead with hanging cured meats casting long shadows downward; the oak dresser with tin dishes is partially visible at left edge; floorboards and white stone floor reflect faint firelight.

Late evening; primary light source is the dying fireplace at right — warm amber glow falls across Heathcliff's extended arm and right cheek, leaving his eyes in dramatic shadow; Hareton is backlit, his silhouette slightly soft against the warm background; cool blue-grey ambient light seeps from the upper stone walls, deepening the tension; shadows stretch long and hard across the floor.

Heathcliff (C1): tall adult male around 40, dark brown skin, short black slightly messy hair, sharp black eyes narrowed with anger, wearing a dark gentleman's coat over a white shirt, dark trousers, leather boots — his full height notably exceeds the door frame nearby by nearly half a head. Hareton (C5): teenage male around 18, thick dark brown messy hair, rough young face showing restrained anxiety, stocky broad-shouldered build, wearing a dark coarse work shirt and trousers, rough hands visible in the hushing gesture.

STYLE_PLACEHOLDER

No text or lettering within the image; no modern objects; maintain period-accurate Victorian Gothic interior; expressions must convey emotional subtext — Hareton's fear-laced caution, Heathcliff's volcanic rage held at the edge of eruption; avoid cartoonish exaggeration.

covered_sentences: Catherine left the room quickly, and Hareton and I followed her.
A moment later, we heard the front door slam.
Heathcliff had gone out, so we could all relax a little.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Wide establishing shot: three figures occupy the parlor interior after Heathcliff's departure — Nelly seated near the fireplace at right, Catherine and Hareton positioned center and center-left respectively; the front door at left stands slightly ajar, implying recent exit; composition breathes with open floor space between the figures to convey released tension.

Catherine (C6b) sits or half-perches on the edge of a dark wooden chair, shoulders dropped from their earlier rigid posture, hands loosely folded in her lap — a subtle exhalation of relief; Hareton (C5) stands nearby with weight shifted to one hip, arms no longer tensed, expression cautiously easing; Nelly (C7) is already settled by the fire with hands resting in her lap, posture calm and watchful — the three share a silent collective breath of relief without overt gesturing.

Wuthering Heights parlor (S1): stone walls, white stone floor, large stone fireplace right with low steady fire now burning more calmly, oak dresser at left with tin and silver dishes, wooden beams overhead; the main front door at left is shown slightly ajar or just closed, a thin sliver of dark night visible at the gap.

Firelight now dominates more warmly without Heathcliff's oppressive presence; the orange glow from the hearth spreads more evenly across the room, softening edges; cooler stone-grey tones still present in walls and floor but tempered by warmth; the mood is quieter amber rather than sharp chiaroscuro — tension releasing into guarded calm.

Catherine (C6b): young woman around 17-18, golden curly hair slightly disheveled, dark eyes, fair but haggard complexion, petite slender frame, plain dark dress past the knee — her suppressed expression beginning to soften. Hareton (C5): teenage male around 18, dark messy hair, rough young face, stocky build, dark coarse work clothes. Nelly Dean (C7): middle-aged woman around 35-45, dark brown hair neatly pinned up, round kind face, stocky medium frame, dark housekeeper dress with white apron.

STYLE_PLACEHOLDER

No text within image; no modern elements; avoid depicting the door slam directly — convey it through the aftermath of stillness; the emotional register is quiet relief, not celebration; maintain Victorian Gothic interior accuracy.

covered_sentences: Catherine and Hareton started reading together as they usually did in the evenings.
I sat sewing by the fire.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Intimate interior three-shot with a sense of domestic routine interrupted: Catherine and Hareton share a book at a small table center-frame, heads leaning slightly toward each other; Nelly sits right foreground near the fire with sewing in her hands; the composition is warm and settled — then the faint implication of an outside presence is introduced through a shadow or the door edge at far left.

Catherine (C6b) and Hareton (C5) sit close together at a table, the open book between them — Hareton's rough finger perhaps tracing a line while Catherine reads softly; their postures show ease and quiet companionship, a habitual evening ritual. Nelly (C7) sits slightly apart near the hearth, needle and thread in motion, glancing toward the reader pair with mild fond attention; no dramatic action — stillness and routine are the subject.

Wuthering Heights parlor (S1): fireplace right with steady warm fire, stone walls, wooden beams, oak dresser left; a small wooden table mid-frame where the young pair sit; the main door at left is closed but its presence is felt — a thin sliver of shadow or the door handle catching light hints at imminent entry.

Warm steady firelight fills the room with golden amber tones — the most comfortable the palette has felt in the scene sequence; the hearth casts soft halos around the three figures; Nelly closest to the fire is warmest-lit, Catherine and Hareton receive secondary warm glow; upper walls and beams remain in cool shadow; the overall mood is brief domestic peace before disruption.

Catherine (C6b): golden curly hair, dark eyes, petite slender frame in plain dark dress, suppressed expression slightly relaxed in the quiet moment — leaning toward the book. Hareton (C5): dark messy hair, stocky broad frame, rough hands on the book page, young face attentive and earnest. Nelly Dean (C7): dark pinned hair, round kind face, dark housekeeper dress with white apron, sewing held in both hands near the fire.

STYLE_PLACEHOLDER

No text or lettering visible; no modern objects; the tone must read as momentary peace — avoid any action or urgency; Hareton and Catherine's shared reading posture should feel habitual and tender without being overtly romantic; the hint of Heathcliff's return should be environmental rather than figurative.

covered_sentences: When he walked into the room, Catherine and Hareton looked up from their book.
I don't know if you have noticed, but their eyes are exactly alike.
They are the first Catherine's eyes.
I suppose Mr. Heathcliff saw the resemblance as he looked at them.
He told them to leave the room, but made a sign to me to stay behind.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Split-focus composition across two beats: PANEL A — full room width, Heathcliff standing in the doorway at left, eyes falling on Catherine and Hareton who look up from their book at center; PANEL B — close two-shot on Catherine's and Hareton's faces side by side, their dark eyes catching the firelight identically, framed to emphasize the uncanny resemblance; Heathcliff's face in soft background blur showing a rare crack in composure.

PANEL A: Heathcliff (C1) has just entered, one hand still on the door frame, his forward momentum paused — his expression shifts from habitual hardness to something involuntary and stricken as his gaze lands on the young pair. Catherine (C6b) and Hareton (C5) look up simultaneously from the book, their faces turned toward him at the same angle — the visual rhyme between their upturned expressions is immediate. PANEL B: tight on the two pairs of eyes — Catherine's and Hareton's dark eyes reflect firelight in the same way, the same shape, the same depth; Heathcliff's hand makes a small dismissive wave gesture toward the door (telling them to leave) while his other hand or a glance directs Nelly to remain.

Wuthering Heights parlor (S1): doorway at left where Heathcliff enters, stone walls, fireplace right casting warm light, oak dresser in background; in PANEL B the background narrows to the stone wall and firelight glow, keeping focus on the faces.

The firelight becomes emotionally charged in this beat — it illuminates Catherine's and Hareton's upturned faces with warm gold, making their identical eyes vivid and unmistakable; Heathcliff is partially backlit from the cold dark doorway, a sliver of night behind him, his face half in warm firelight half in shadow — the light itself marks his psychological rupture; the room's warm amber takes on a ghostly undertone.

Heathcliff (C1): tall adult male around 40, dark brown skin, short black slightly messy hair, sharp black eyes — in this moment those eyes show involuntary pain rather than anger, dark gentleman's coat, leather boots, head exceeds door frame by half a head. Catherine (C6b): young woman around 17-18, golden curly hair, dark eyes looking up — her eyes are the focal point of PANEL B. Hareton (C5): teenage male around 18, dark messy hair, rough young face, dark eyes looking up beside Catherine's — the resemblance to old Catherine must be visually legible. Nelly Dean (C7): present near the fire at right, her gaze moving between Heathcliff and the young pair, observing everything.

STYLE_PLACEHOLDER

No text within the image; the eyes of Catherine and Hareton must be rendered with enough detail to communicate their identical quality — this is the emotional pivot of the scene; Heathcliff's involuntary emotional response must be subtle, not melodramatic; avoid depicting Nelly as a passive prop — she should read as an active witness.
